zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Colwick於1324年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾時，她的父親Sir William de Colewick II25歲，母親Joan Peche4歲。她於1375年與Sir Richard Byron de Clayton在英國越可爾布拉德福德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她於1418年住在英國爾布雷雷頓 姆 斯維其可。她去世於1426年10月8日在英國越可爾克雷頓享年102歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾可爾維其可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Colwick于1324年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾时，她的父亲Sir William de Colewick II25岁，母亲Joan Peche4岁。她于1375年与Sir Richard Byron de Clayton在英国越可爾布拉德福德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她于1418年住在英国爾布雷雷頓 姆 斯維其可。她去世于1426年10月8日在英国越可爾克雷頓享年102岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾可爾維其可。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Colwick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Colwick (Nottinghamshire), but in the West Midlands perhaps alternatively from Colewick (lost, in Saint Johns in Bedwardine, Worcestershire) or Colewich (Staffordshire), for which see also Colledge . The placenames are formed either with the Old English personal name Cola or with Old English col ‘coal, charcoal’ + Old English wic ‘dairy farm’ (see Wick ). The surname Colwick is no longer found in Britain. Compare Collick .

variants.name: Collick
meanings: nickname from Middle English collok ‘tankard, bowl, or pail’.

habitational name from any of several places, including Colhook Common in Petworth (Sussex), Colwick in Nottinghamshire (see   Colwick  ), and perhaps a lost or unidentified place in Somerset or Devon.

variants.name: Colledge
meanings: English (Midlands and northern England): habitational name from Colwich in Staffordshire, named from Old English col ‘(char)coal’ + wīc ‘building’. The name has become associated with the familiar word college.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Colwick was born in 1324, in Nottinghamshire, England, her father, Sir William de Colewick II, was 25 and her mother, Joan Peche, was 4. She married Sir Richard Byron de Clayton in 1375, in Bradford, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She lived in Brereton cum Smethwick, Cheshire, England in 1418. She died on 8 October 1426, in Clayton, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 102, and was buried in Colwick, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Colwick est née en 1324, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Colewick II, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Joan Peche, avait 4 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Richard Byron de Clayton en 1375, à Bradford, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle a vécu à Brereton cum Smethwick, Cheshire, Angleterre en 1418. Elle est décédée le 8 octobre 1426, à Clayton, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 102 ans, et a été enterrée à Colwick, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Colwick 1324 in Nottinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William de Colewick II 25 und ihre Mutter Joan Peche 4. Sie hat 1375 in Bradford, Yorkshire, England Sir Richard Byron de Clayton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. 1418 hat sie in Brereton cum Smethwick, Cheshire, England gelebt. Sie starb am 8. Oktober 1426 im Alter von 102 Jahren in Clayton, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Colwick, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Colwick è nata il nel 1324 nel Nottinghamshire, England, sua padre, Sir William de Colewick II, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Joan Peche, aveva 4 anni. Lei sposato Sir Richard Byron de Clayton nel 1375, nel Bradford, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Vissuto a Brereton cum Smethwick, Cheshire, England nel 1418. Morì il 8 ottobre 1426, nel Clayton, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 102 anni, e fu sepolta a Colwick, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Colwickが1324年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir William de Colewick IIは25歳，母Joan Pecheは4歳であった。1375年にイギリスヨークシュアイアブラドフォードでSir Richard Byron de Claytonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1418年にはイギリスチェシュアイアブレレトン クウム スメスウィックに住んでいた。彼女は1426年10月8日にイギリスヨークシュアイアクレイトンで102歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアコルウィックにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Colwick(이)가 1324년 영국 노틴그함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de Colewick II(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Peche(은)는 4세였다. 그녀는 1375년 영국 요르크실르 브래드퍼드에서 Sir Richard Byron de Clayton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1418년도에 영국 체실르 브레레턴 c 스멧h윅에서 살았다. 그녀는 1426년 10월 8일 영국 요르크실르 클레이턴에서 102세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 코윅에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Colwick nasceu em 1324, em Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Colewick II, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Joan Peche, tinha 4 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Richard Byron de Clayton em 1375, em Bradford, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela viveu em Brereton cum Smethwick, Cheshire, Inglaterra em 1418. Ela faleceu em 8 de outubro de 1426, em Clayton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 102 anos, e foi sepultada em Colwick, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Colwick родилась в 1324 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William de Colewick II было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Peche, 4. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard Byron de Clayton в 1375 г. в местности брадфорд, еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она жила в местности бриретон кум сметвикк, чешир, Англия в 1418 году. Она умерла 8 октября 1426 г. в местности клеитон, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 102 лет и была похоронена в местности колвикк, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Colwick nació en 1324, en Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de Colewick II, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Joan Peche, tenía 4 años. Se casó con Sir Richard Byron de Clayton en 1375, en Bradford, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Vivió en Brereton cum Smethwick, Cheshire, Inglaterra en 1418. Murió el 8 de octubre de 1426, en Clayton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 102 años, y fue sepultada en Colwick, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
